Topic: Value of Standardization & Visibility Across Locations
Quote: "If I have the responsibility of growing sales, I need to know what’s actually being sold. Before Method, we had no clean way to see what products were moving because every franchise was running their own transactions in QuickBooks with their own item names. Now, we have standardized item names in Method, and for the first time, we can get real reports."
Why This Quote Works:
Speaks to multi-location businesses struggling with inconsistent data.
 Highlights Method’s ability to centralize reporting despite franchise autonomy.

Topic: Moving from Fragmented Systems to a Unified CRM
Quote: "Each location was doing their own thing—some were using QuickBooks, some were still on paper. We needed one system to bring it all together. Method let us standardize sales transactions and workflows, while still integrating with QuickBooks where we needed it."
Why This Quote Works:
Hits the pain point of franchises using multiple systems (or no system at all).
 Emphasizes Method’s ability to unify operations without ripping out QuickBooks.

Topic: Compliance & Adoption in a Franchise Model
Quote: "We track compliance now—who’s using Method correctly and who isn’t. Right now, we’re at 66% adoption, and I expect that to go up. Some of them still prefer QuickBooks or paper, but at the end of the day, there’s a lot of value in what we’re doing. They just need to get on board."
Why This Quote Works:
Compliance tracking is a huge concern for franchise operations.
 Acknowledges resistance to change, which is relatable for multi-entity businesses adopting a new system.

Quote: "QuickBooks could process transactions, but it couldn’t give us visibility across locations. Method extended QuickBooks for us—it became our standardized POS and CRM, so now we actually control the data that comes in."
Why This Quote Works:
Addresses one of Method’s biggest selling points: enhancing QuickBooks, not replacing it.
 Appeals to CFOs and franchise owners who worry about QuickBooks limitations in multi-location setups.

Quote: "When I first looked at Method, I didn’t like it. But once I realized how customizable it was, I saw that we could actually build exactly what we needed. We took our ideal reports and worked backwards, designing workflows that made sense for us."
Why This Quote Works:
Hits on common skepticism about off-the-shelf CRMs.
 Reinforces Method’s flexibility for multi-entity businesses with unique needs.

Topic: Why Multi-Entity Companies Need a Defined Process Before Implementing Tech
Quote: "The biggest challenge for any company choosing software is that technology alone doesn’t fix bad processes. You have to define your workflows first—then automate them. If you don’t do that, you’re just automating chaos."
Why This Quote Works:
Speaks to business operators who’ve failed CRM implementations before.
 Positions Method as a tool that fits the business—not the other way around.

Topic: Managing Multi-Location Sales & Operations in a Growing Business
Quote: "We’re going from 50 to 150 locations in the next few years. If we don’t have standardized processes and reporting in place now, that growth is going to be painful. Method gives us the foundation to scale."
Why This Quote Works:
Appeals to franchise owners and multi-location businesses looking to scale efficiently.
 Highlights Method as a long-term growth solution, not just a short-term fix.

Topic: On Tailoring Workflows to Franchise Needs
Quote: “We took our ideal reports and worked backwards, designing workflows that made sense for us.”
